mechanism_narrative: A lower federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, which raises equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward regression beta of FEDFUNDS on 20-day SPX returns is positive and statistically significant at the 5% level, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating household income and slowing consumption, which expands the equity risk premium and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the trigger condition is met, or the mean return conditional on the trigger is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based demand strength across consumer and investment sectors, which boosts aggregate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and HOUST changes adds no incremental explanatory power in a regression (p > 0.05), or positive returns occur in fewer than 55% of joint-signal observations.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a slowdown in industrial production growth signals capacity constraints and margin compression, which reduces earnings forecasts and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of late-cycle instances when INDPRO growth decelerates, or Rank IC over any 3-year window is above -0.03.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising policy rates and elevated consumer price inflation raises real borrowing costs sharply, tightening financial conditions and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: A regression including the FEDFUNDS-CPIAUCSL interaction shows no significant negative coefficient on S&P 500 60-day returns (p > 0.05), or Rank IC falls below -0.03 in fewer than half of rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: In post-recession recoveries, consecutive monthly increases in nonfarm payroll employment signal accelerating labor market absorption, which lifts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered post-recession instances, or the mean conditional return is not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp two-month contraction in industrial production signals a downturn in goods-producing sector activity that precedes weaker corporate revenues, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or the conditional mean return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in advance retail sales reflects robust consumer spending momentum that supports corporate top-line forecasts, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of high-momentum RSAFS months, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
